message:
feat(lang): enable let_chains nightly feature

Added #![feature(let_chains)] to enable Rust's let_chains syntax, which allows
chaining multiple let patterns with boolean conditions using && operators. This
feature is currently unstable and requires the nightly toolchain specified in
rust-toolchain.toml.

The let_chains feature enables cleaner conditional logic patterns like:
  if let Some(x) = opt && x > 5 { ... }
  while let Some(item) = iter.next() && item.is_valid() { ... }

This is more ergonomic than nested if-let or match statements and improves
code readability when dealing with multiple conditional patterns. While not
currently used in the codebase, this feature gate is being enabled proactively
to support future code that would benefit from this pattern.

Rationale:
- Aligns with project's use of Rust 2024 nightly features
- Provides more expressive pattern matching capabilities
- Reduces nesting in conditional logic
- Part of Rust's path to stable (RFC 2497)

Note: This feature is enabled at the crate root and available throughout the
entire codebase. The project already requires nightly per rust-toolchain.toml,
so this adds no new toolchain constraints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
